summary: Full regression on Joxera vibrant landing page + Emergent Google auth for the new preview host (7a29f715-...). All 5 backend contract tests pass and all 15 landing-page/auth playwright checks pass. Two critical environment-config regressions were fixed before testing (ALLOWED_HOSTS + missing frontend VITE_API_URL) — see rca. Post-fix, authenticated users correctly see the sign-in card and Google buttons hidden.

issues: Non-blocking pageerror 'Incorrect locale information provided' (also flagged in iteration_1). Likely Intl API called with an unsupported/empty locale — recommend try/catch fallback to 'en-US'.

action_items: Persist the two env fixes into repo/setup so they survive fresh spins: (a) backend/.env ALLOWED_HOSTS should include the preview host (or wildcard '.preview.emergentagent.com') and (b) frontend/.env should provide VITE_API_URL pointing at the preview backend, e.g. https://<host>/api. Without these, both frontend->backend calls and Django host validation break on any new preview URL.

Investigate the persistent 'Incorrect locale information provided' pageerror thrown on landing and /auth/login (likely Intl.* called with an unsupported locale via i18n). Non-blocking but pollutes console/Sentry.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/services/api/client.ts falls back to 'http://localhost:8000/api' when VITE_API_URL is unset — that fallback is unreachable from the deployed preview URL and produces silent failures (login form shows 'Something went wrong' with no useful error). Consider throwing at startup when VITE_API_URL is missing, or defaulting to same-origin '/api' so the app degrades gracefully.

backend/.env has no ALLOWED_HOSTS entry — settings.py defaults to only 'localhost,127.0.0.1', so every external request to the preview URL was rejected with DisallowedHost before my fix. Recommend adding ALLOWED_HOSTS to setup_env.sh or defaulting to '*' in DEBUG.

GoogleLoginButton assigns window.location.href — fine, but it also fires on every click without a debounce; double-clicks queue two navigations (mostly harmless because the browser only honours the last).
Landing page uses `useLocationContext()` which is called before any auth check; if the location context throws, the whole landing page dies — worth wrapping in an error boundary.
GoogleSessionView still stores JWTs client-side in localStorage after callback (XSS-vulnerable); pre-existing, but worth noting again.
seed_data_creation: None. Backend tests only exercise idempotent read/login endpoints; no rows created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL is https://7a29f715-0a3a-453f-a736-5a71dd94bc81.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend runs uvicorn on :8001, frontend Vite on :3000 (supervisor). Backend rejects hosts not in ALLOWED_HOSTS — that env var was empty in this environment and had to be added to /app/backend/.env (see rca). Frontend has no .env in the repo so VITE_API_URL was undefined and defaulted to http://localhost:8000/api — I created /app/frontend/.env pointing at the preview /api. New tests live at /app/tests/test_landing_google_auth_e2e.py (kept out of /app/backend/tests/ because that dir's conftest.py needs pytest-django which isn't installed). Run: `cd /app && python -m pytest tests/test_landing_google_auth_e2e.py -v`. Google OAuth cannot be end-to-end tested (real popup); we only assert the button navigates to https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=<encoded /auth/callback>.

issue_1.root_cause: backend/.env had no ALLOWED_HOSTS; config/settings.py falls back to ['localhost','127.0.0.1']. The forwarded host from Cloudflare ('7a29f715-...preview.emergentagent.com') was rejected.

issue_2.symptom: Login form on /auth/login showed 'Something went wrong'; authenticated landing state never triggered even after injecting valid JWTs.
issue_2.root_cause: No /app/frontend/.env existed, so import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL was undefined and client.ts fell back to 'http://localhost:8000/api'. From the browser at the preview URL that host is unreachable, so every axios call (including /auth/me/) failed silently, keeping isAuthenticated=false.
issue_2.fix: Created /app/frontend/.env with VITE_API_URL=https://<preview-host>/api and restarted frontend. Verified /auth/me/ requests now hit the preview host and the landing page correctly hides the sign-in card when authed.
